summary: Added the smallest explicit host hash canary that proves real Chromium Dedicated Worker and packaged Electron renderer/Worker execution against the Node baseline for the M2 hello, command/query, and save/load hashes. The browser-side canary is a dedicated-worker page in apps/web, and the packaged Electron app loads that same built page only when MONSOON_HOST_HASH_CANARY=1. Security posture stayed intact: nodeIntegration remains off, preload authority stayed narrow, and no new IPC or filesystem capability was introduced.

tests_run.result: Pre-existing orchestration files were already dirty and left untouched. Current worktree shows the canary source files, desktop/web build changes, apps/web/package.json, pnpm-lock.yaml, and the new outbox handoff.

Node hash 61e0ba1c matched Worker-compatible hash 61e0ba1c; command/query hash d275faa4 matched Worker-compatible command/query hash d275faa4; save/load hash 28074edd matched Worker-compatible save/load hash 28074edd; save/load byte length 10920.
Vite built dist/index.html and dist/host-hash-canary.html plus the host-hash-canary worker bundle and shared assets.
Electron security preferences passed; preload exposes only the named monsoonDesktop bridge; renderer navigation is restricted to the packaged renderer root.
Packaged main, preload, renderer, and platform adapter artifacts to .tmp/desktop-package/MonsoonSovereigns-win32-x64.

Command: pnpm --filter @monsoon/sim-runner sim:host-hash-canary -- --browser chromium. Node baseline hello 61e0ba1c, command/query d275faa4, save/load 28074edd, save byte length 10920. Chromium dedicated worker matched hello 61e0ba1c, command/query d275faa4, save/load 28074edd, save byte length 10920.

Command: pnpm --filter @monsoon/sim-runner sim:host-hash-canary -- --browser electron. Node baseline hello 61e0ba1c, command/query d275faa4, save/load 28074edd, save byte length 10920. Electron renderer dedicated worker matched hello 61e0ba1c, command/query d275faa4, save/load 28074edd, save byte length 10920.

tests_run.command: node -e JSON parse/shape check for M2-HOST-HASH-CANARY-001
tests_run.result: Task JSON shape ok: M2-HOST-HASH-CANARY-001 IN_PROGRESS 16 13 10

decisions: Kept the canary browser-safe by putting the shared hash computation in apps/web/src/host-hash-canary/compute-host-hash-canary.ts and reusing it from the dedicated worker and the Node runner.
Made apps/web a two-entry Vite build with base ./ so the canary can run from both the web dev build and the packaged Electron file URL without adding a new runtime dependency.
Copied apps/web/dist into the packaged desktop renderer under renderer/web so the existing secure Electron main process can load host-hash-canary.html only when MONSOON_HOST_HASH_CANARY=1 is set.
Kept the browser/desktop comparison in the Node runner script so the page can stay minimal and the exact commands and hashes are printed in test output.
risks: desktop:package now depends on build:web having already produced apps/web/dist; if the web build is skipped or stale, the packaged canary page will be absent.
Chromium file:// execution depends on relative asset URLs and allow-file-access-from-files; the build uses base ./ to keep those paths stable.
The task and goal/model orchestration files were already dirty before this work and were intentionally left untouched.
